questions.q: In human beings, disorder caused due to trisomy of 21st chromosome is:
questions.exp: Trisomy of chromosome 21 causes Down Syndrome. Turner Syndrome involves 45,X; Klinefelter is 47,XXY.

questions.q: National Health Policy 2017 identified co-ordinated action on seven priority areas for improving the health. Which option is NOT included under the seven priority areas?
questions.exp: NHP 2017 seven priority areas include Swachh Bharat, Yatri Suraksha, Nirbhaya Nari etc. 'Annapurna Scheme' is not among the seven priority areas.

questions.q: Which pulse causes lathyrism when consumed on a regular basis?
questions.exp: Khesari dal (Lathyrus sativus) contains ODAP, a neurotoxin that causes lathyrism — a paralytic disorder of lower limbs.

questions.q: In 2017, which Satellite Launch Vehicle of ISRO successfully launched 104 satellites in a single flight?
questions.exp: PSLV-C37 launched 104 satellites on 15 February 2017, setting a world record.

questions.q: Ozone layer thickness is measured in:
questions.exp: The thickness of the ozone layer is measured in Dobson Units (DU).

questions.q: The number of cervical vertebrae in a human body is:
questions.exp: There are 7 cervical vertebrae (C1-C7) in the human neck. 12 are thoracic and 5 are lumbar.

questions.q: In which year was India's first interplanetary mission to planet Mars launched successfully?
questions.exp: India's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was launched on 5 November 2013 and entered Mars orbit on 24 September 2014.

questions.q: When did the Government of India pass the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act?
questions.exp: The Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act was passed in 1974. The Air Act was 1981 and EPA was 1986.

questions.q: According to SDG India Index 2018, SDG 1, SDG 2 and SDG 9 represent respectively:
opts: No poverty; Zero hunger; Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure
No poverty; Quality education; Life on land
Clean water and sanitation; Climate action; Sustainable cities and communities
Gender equality; Climate action; Zero hunger
questions.exp: SDG 1 = No Poverty, SDG 2 = Zero Hunger, SDG 9 = Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.

questions.q: In the aquatic ecosystem of a pond, which of the following components is represented by fungi, bacteria and flagellates?
questions.exp: Fungi, bacteria and flagellates are decomposers (saprotrophs) that break down dead organic matter and recycle nutrients.

questions.q: In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court of India declare the prevalence of Fundamental Rights over Directive Principles of State Policy in case of conflict between the two?
opts: The State of Madras v. Champakam Dorairajan (1951)
Kesavananda Bharati v. the State of Kerala (1973)
Minerva Mills v. the Union of India (1980)
questions.exp: In State of Madras v. Champakam Dorairajan (1951), the SC held that Fundamental Rights prevail over DPSPs, leading to the 1st Constitutional Amendment.

questions.q: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the Election Commission of India?
opts: In case of differences of opinion amongst the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners, the matter is decided by the Law Commission.
The Election Commissioners cannot be removed from their office except on the recommendation of the Chief Election Commissioner.
The Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners enjoy equal powers.
The term of office of Election Commissioners is six years from the date they assume office or till the day they attain the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.
questions.exp: Differences of opinion in Election Commission are decided by majority — not by the Law Commission, which has no role in EC decisions.

questions.q: An amendment of the Constitution shall require to be ratified by the legislatures of not less than one-half of the states if such amendment seeks to make changes in:
questions.exp: Article 279A (GST Council) requires ratification by at least half the state legislatures when amended, as it affects the federal financial structure.

questions.q: Which among the following Article speaks about impeachment of the President of India?
questions.exp: Article 61 of the Indian Constitution deals with the procedure for impeachment of the President.

questions.q: As per Article 76 of the Indian Constitution, the President shall appoint a person to be Attorney General for India:
opts: who is qualified to be appointed a Judge of the High Court.
who has been an advocate of the Supreme Court for at least seven years.
who is qualified to be appointed a Judge of the Supreme Court.
who has been an advocate of the High Court for at least seven years.
questions.exp: Article 76 states the AG must be qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court.

questions.q: K.M. Munshi was related to:
questions.exp: K.M. Munshi was a member of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution, headed by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.

questions.q: Under whose advice can the President of India declare Emergency under Article 352?
questions.exp: The 44th Amendment (1978) made it mandatory that the President declares Emergency only on written advice of the Union Cabinet.

questions.q: The Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Good Governance and Policy Analysis is in:
questions.exp: The Atal Bihari Vajpayee Institute of Good Governance and Policy Analysis (AIGGPA) is located in Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. It shall be the duty of the Advocate General to give advice to the Government of the State upon legal matters.
2. The Advocate General shall hold office during the pleasure of the Cabinet.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.exp: Statement 1 is correct (Article 165). Statement 2 is incorrect — the AG holds office during the pleasure of the Governor, not the Cabinet.

questions.q: The list of powers, authority and responsibilities which may be entrusted to the Panchayats is given in the:
questions.exp: The Eleventh Schedule (added by 73rd Amendment, 1992) contains the 29 matters in which Panchayats may be entrusted with powers. Twelfth Schedule deals with Municipalities.

questions.q: Bhimma tribe lives in which of the districts of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: The Bhimma tribe is primarily found in Betul and Chhindwara districts of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Mandwara festival is celebrated by the:
questions.exp: Mandwara is a traditional festival celebrated by the Bhariya tribe of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Mahanayak 'Tantya Mama' is related to which tribal group?
questions.exp: Tantya Bhil (Tantya Mama) was a legendary hero of the Bhil community who fought against British rule in the 19th century.

questions.q: Freedom fighter Raghunath Singh Mandloi is related to the:
questions.exp: Raghunath Singh Mandloi was a freedom fighter from the Bhilala tribe of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Who is late Shri Badal Bhoi?
questions.exp: Badal Bhoi was a famous tribal freedom fighter from Madhya Pradesh who participated in the 1857 revolt and resistance movements.

questions.q: Which award is given to women for tribal, folk and traditional arts by the Madhya Pradesh Government?
questions.exp: The National Devi Ahilya Award (Rashtriya Devi Ahilya Samman) is given by the MP Government to women for excellence in tribal, folk and traditional arts.

questions.q: Birha is the popular folk song of which group of tribal women?
questions.exp: Birha is a traditional folk song associated with Kol tribal women of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Which award is given for writings on the cultural tradition and specialities of tribal life in Indian literature?
opts: Veer Shankar Shah - Raghunath Shah National Award
questions.exp: The Thakkar Bapa National Award is given for outstanding writings on the cultural traditions and specialties of tribal life in Indian literature.

questions.q: In whose name is the Self-Employment Scheme being run for tribals in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: The self-employment scheme for tribals in MP runs in the name of Tantya Bhil (Tantya Mama), the legendary tribal hero.

questions.q: The year of establishment of 'Vanya', the publishing house of Tribal Affairs Department is:
questions.exp: 'Vanya', the publication wing of the Tribal Affairs Department of MP, was established in 1975 to promote tribal literature and culture.

questions.q: What was the lower ceiling of Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) which was amended in 2007 by the Government of India?
questions.exp: The Banking Regulation Act was amended in 2007 to set the SLR floor at 24%. Previously it was 25%.

questions.q: What was the proportion of external debt in the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at the end of September 2023?
questions.exp: India's external debt to GDP ratio was approximately 18.6% at the end of September 2023 per Ministry of Finance data.

questions.q: What percentage of all factories in India were located in Madhya Pradesh in the year 2017-18?
questions.exp: According to Annual Survey of Industries 2017-18, approximately 2% of all registered factories in India were located in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Where and when was the first G20 Summit held?
questions.exp: The first G20 Leaders' Summit was held in Washington D.C., USA in November 2008 to address the global financial crisis.

questions.q: UNNATI (Agri-GIS) project of Madhya Pradesh Government is for:
opts: Providing financial assistance to farmers for purchasing smartphones.
Leveraging technology like satellite imagery and drone data to facilitate informed decision-making in agriculture.
Promoting the cultivation of genetically modified crops.
Establishing a network of agricultural universities across the state.
questions.exp: UNNATI (Agri-GIS) leverages satellite imagery, drone data and geospatial technology for informed agricultural decision-making in MP.

questions.q: The Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan has emerged from the amalgamation of which of the following schemes?
opts: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA) and National Skill Development Mission (NSDM)
Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA) and Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Literacy Mission (PMGDLM)
Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA) and Teacher Education (TE)
Beti Bachao Beti Padhao Abhiyan and Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
questions.exp: Samagra Shiksha (2018) merged three schemes: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA), and Teacher Education (TE).

questions.q: In the year 2022-23, on comparing the per capita income of Madhya Pradesh and that of India on the basis of advanced estimates at constant (2011-12) prices, we find that:
opts: Per capita income of Madhya Pradesh is higher than that of India.
Per capita income of Madhya Pradesh is lower than that of India.
Per capita income of Madhya Pradesh is equal to that of India.
questions.exp: Madhya Pradesh's per capita income is lower than India's national average. MP's per capita GSDP remains below the national per capita GDP.

questions.q: GI tagged Chinnor rice is primarily produced in which district of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: Chinnor rice, a fragrant variety with GI tag, is primarily grown in Chhindwara and Jabalpur districts of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: According to NITI Aayog's SDG India Index 2020, how are the different categories of States' performance in Sustainable Development Goals defined on the basis of index score range?
questions.exp: SDG India Index 2020: Aspirant (0-49), Performer (50-64), Front-runner (65-99), Achiever (100).

questions.q: The central part of the UNIX Operating System is:
questions.exp: The Kernel is the core/central part of the UNIX operating system managing hardware resources, processes and memory.

questions.q: A _________ attack comes in the form of deceptive emails or text messages that may ask you to install software or divulge personal information.
questions.exp: Phishing is a cyberattack using deceptive emails or messages to trick users into revealing personal info or installing malware.

questions.q: Kurrently is a/an:
questions.exp: Kurrently is a real-time search engine that searches across social media platforms for real-time updates.

questions.q: The 'Imitation Game' was the original name of:
questions.exp: Alan Turing originally called the Turing Test the 'Imitation Game' in his 1950 paper 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence'.

questions.q: The founder of Twitter is:
questions.exp: Twitter was co-founded by Jack Dorsey, Noah Glass, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams in 2006. Jack Dorsey is the primary founder who sent the first tweet.

questions.q: What are the four pillars of E-Governance?
questions.exp: The four pillars of E-Governance are: People, Process, Technology, and Government.

questions.q: A social networking site for professionals and business people who wish to connect with other professionals is:
questions.exp: LinkedIn is the professional networking platform designed for professionals and business people.

questions.q: The branch of Artificial Intelligence is:
questions.exp: Machine Learning is a branch/subset of Artificial Intelligence.

questions.q: MPEG stands for:
questions.exp: MPEG stands for Moving Picture Experts Group — an ISO/IEC working group developing audio/video compression standards.

questions.q: In the field of Internet, W3C stands for:
questions.exp: W3C stands for World Wide Web Consortium — founded by Tim Berners-Lee to develop open web standards.

questions.q: According to 2021-22, the area and production of wheat occupies which place among cereals, in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: In 2021-22, wheat occupies First position among cereals in Madhya Pradesh in both area and production.

questions.q: In which district of Madhya Pradesh was the production of soyabean initially started?
questions.exp: Soybean cultivation was first introduced in Indore district of MP. MP is now known as the 'Soy Bowl of India'.

questions.q: In which of the following industry is rock phosphate used?
questions.exp: Rock phosphate (Ca3(PO4)2) is the primary raw material for phosphatic fertilizer production.

questions.q: The south-eastern part of Madhya Pradesh receives maximum rainfall from:
opts: Bay of Bengal branch of monsoon
questions.exp: The south-eastern MP receives maximum rainfall from the Bay of Bengal branch of the South-West monsoon.

questions.q: Black soil in Madhya Pradesh is formed by which rock?
questions.exp: Black cotton soil (regur) in MP is derived from weathering of Basaltic (Deccan Trap) rocks.

questions.q: When was bamboo nationalized in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: Bamboo was nationalized in Madhya Pradesh in 1963, with state government taking over bamboo collection rights in forest areas.

questions.q: The southern part of the Vindhyan Scarpland is called:
questions.exp: The Bhander plateau is situated in the southern part of the Vindhyan Scarpland of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd., Bhopal was established with the support of a company of which country?
questions.exp: BHEL Bhopal was established in 1956 with technical collaboration from the Soviet Union (Russia) — a Nehruvian era PSU.

questions.q: According to 2011 Census, which district had the highest percentage of urban population?
questions.exp: According to Census 2011, Bhopal district had the highest percentage of urban population in MP (~69.4%).

questions.q: In which of the following irrigation projects do four states have partnership?
questions.exp: Bansagar Dam Project on Sone River is a joint project of MP, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Jharkhand — four states.

questions.q: What is the name of the author of the historical novel 'Durgesh Nandini' written in Bengali language?
questions.exp: 'Durgesh Nandini' (1865) is the first Bengali novel, written by Bankim Chandra Chatterjee.

questions.q: Who appointed Keshav Chandra Sen as the Chief Acharya of Brahmo Samaj?
questions.exp: Devendranath Thakur appointed Keshav Chandra Sen as the Chief Acharya of Brahmo Samaj in 1857.

questions.q: During which movement did Gandhiji call for boycott of the Prince of Wales?
questions.exp: During the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920-22), Gandhi called for boycott of the Prince of Wales's visit to India in November 1921.

questions.q: In the administrative system of the Mauryan period, Pradeshta is related to which department?
questions.exp: In Mauryan administration, 'Pradeshta' was a revenue official responsible for revenue collection in the provinces.

questions.q: For whom did K.M. Panikkar say that 'He was the first diplomat of modern India'?
questions.exp: K.M. Panikkar described Gopal Krishna Gokhale as 'the first diplomat of modern India' for his skilled negotiations with British officials.

questions.q: In which Vedas are Sabha and Samiti included as separate institutions?
questions.exp: The Atharvaveda contains references to Sabha and Samiti as two distinct popular assemblies/institutions of the Vedic period.

questions.q: There was a provision for reciting which mantra in the Upanayana ritual of the Kshatriya castes?
questions.exp: The Trishtubh mantra was prescribed for the Upanayana of Kshatriya castes; Gayatri for Brahmins and Jagati for Vaishyas.

questions.q: Where is the most famous Kailash temple located among the cave temples?
questions.exp: The famous Kailasa Temple (Cave 16) is at Ellora, Maharashtra — carved from a single rock by the Rashtrakuta king Krishna I.

questions.q: Which of the following Vedas was the earliest composition?
questions.exp: The Rigveda is the oldest of the four Vedas (~1500-1200 BCE) and the oldest religious text in the world still in use.

questions.q: What is called Antaranga Sadhana under Ashtanga Yoga?
questions.exp: Under Patanjali's Ashtanga Yoga, Dharana (concentration), Dhyana (meditation) and Samadhi are called Antaranga Sadhana (inner limbs). Dharana begins this antaranga path.

questions.q: Which of the following two States of India have more than 50 percent of the total country's coal reserves?\n(i) Jharkhand  (ii) Madhya Pradesh  (iii) Odisha  (iv) Chhattisgarh
questions.exp: Jharkhand (~26%) and Odisha (~25%) together hold more than 50% of India's total coal reserves.

questions.q: In the year 2021-22, which State of India had the first rank in production of pulses?
questions.exp: Madhya Pradesh ranked first in pulse production in 2021-22, contributing about 23% of India's total pulse output.

questions.q: Out of these, which State has the largest deposits of Bauxite?
questions.exp: Odisha has the largest bauxite deposits in India (>50% of national reserves), concentrated in Koraput and Kalahandi districts.

questions.q: Which of the following statements is NOT correct about El Nino?
opts: El Nino is an ocean current, which appears off the Peru coast.
El Nino weakens the Indian Monsoon.
La Nina strengthens the Indian Monsoon.
questions.exp: El Nino does NOT occur every year — it is irregular, occurring every 2-7 years. The other three statements are correct.

questions.q: From the following States of India, which was the largest producer of Rice in the year 2021-22?
questions.exp: West Bengal is traditionally the largest producer of rice in India and retained its top position in 2021-22.

questions.q: Which of the following is the longest river?
questions.exp: The Narmada (1,312 km) is the longest among the options. It is the longest west-flowing river in India.

questions.q: From the following, which cyclone struck the western coast of India in the year 2021?
questions.exp: Cyclone Tauktae struck India's western coast in May 2021, making landfall in Gujarat. Amphan (2020) hit eastern coast; Michaung (2023) hit eastern coast; Fani (2019) hit Odisha.

questions.q: Arrange the following mountain peaks in ascending order according to their height:\n(i) Guru Shikhar  (ii) Mahendragiri  (iii) Anaimudi  (iv) Panchmarhi
questions.exp: Heights: Panchmarhi (1352m) < Mahendragiri (~1501m) < Guru Shikhar (1722m) < Anaimudi (2695m). Ascending: (iv), (ii), (i), (iii).

questions.q: According to the Census year of 2011, which of the following States of India had the lowest density of population?
questions.exp: Arunachal Pradesh had the lowest population density in India per Census 2011 at just 17 persons per sq km.

questions.q:
Which of the following statements are correct about Rani Durgavati Shri Anna Protsahan Yojana?
1. It promotes millets in Madhya Pradesh.
2. It covers the whole of Madhya Pradesh.
3. This scheme is for the period of 2023-24 to 2025-26.
questions.exp: Rani Durgavati Shri Anna Protsahan Yojana promotes millets across all of MP for 2023-24 to 2025-26. All three statements are correct.

questions.q: Who among the following was nominated to the Rajya Sabha by the President of India as the 12th member in the year 2024?
questions.exp: Sudha Murty, noted author and philanthropist, was nominated to the Rajya Sabha by President Droupadi Murmu in March 2024.

questions.q: Who is the para-canoe athlete from Madhya Pradesh who was awarded the Arjuna Award in 2023?
questions.exp: Prachi Yadav, a para-canoe athlete from MP, received the Arjuna Award in 2023 for outstanding achievements in para-sports.

questions.q: Who among the following is associated with traditional Maach singing style?
questions.exp: Om Prakash Sharma is renowned for the traditional Maach folk theatre and singing style of MP's Malwa region.

questions.q: Who is the recipient of the 2023 International Prize in Statistics equivalent to the Nobel Prize in Mathematics?
questions.exp: C.R. Rao (Calyampudi Radhakrishna Rao), the legendary Indian-American statistician, received the 2023 International Prize in Statistics.

questions.q: Who, in the year 2024, became the youngest player ever to win the FIDE Candidates Tournament?
questions.exp: Dommaraju Gukesh (17 years) became the youngest player to win the FIDE Candidates Tournament 2024 in Toronto, earning the right to challenge Ding Liren for the World Chess Championship.

questions.q: In which district is Lamheta village located, where the Geo Park is set to be established?
questions.exp: Lamheta village on the Narmada banks in Jabalpur district is known for dinosaur fossils and is the proposed site for a UNESCO Geo Park.

questions.q: Who among the following has been appointed as the Chairperson of Lokpal of India in 2024?
questions.exp: Justice Ajay Manikrao Khanwilkar, former Supreme Court judge, was appointed as Lokpal Chairperson in 2024.

questions.q: Where is the world's first 'Vedic Clock' installed?
questions.exp: The world's first 'Vedic Clock' (based on ancient Indian time system) was installed at Jantar Mantar in Ujjain, MP.

questions.q: The winner of the five test matches cricket series held between India and England from January to March, 2024 was:
questions.exp: India won the 5-Test series against England (January-March 2024) by 4-1, with England winning only the 1st Test at Hyderabad.

questions.q: Where was the first literary reference to Narmada river found?
questions.exp: The first literary reference to the Narmada (as Reva) is found in the Shatapatha Brahmana, one of the oldest Sanskrit prose texts.

questions.q: Which Chalcolithic site is also called the birth place of Varahamihira?
questions.exp: Dangwada in Ujjain district, MP is a Chalcolithic site traditionally considered the birthplace of astronomer Varahamihira (5th-6th century CE).

questions.q: Which of the following pairs is false? (Sanctuary — District)
questions.exp: Ghatigaon Wildlife Sanctuary is in Gwalior district, NOT Indore. Other pairs (Bori-Hoshangabad, Gangau-Panna, Karera-Shivpuri) are correct.

questions.q: With which genre is Shankar Rao Pandit associated?
questions.exp: Pandit Shankar Rao Pandit was a legendary Khayal vocalist of the Gwalior Gharana of Hindustani classical music.

questions.q: What is the Sutradhar of Gammat folk theatre called?
questions.exp: In Gammat, the traditional folk theatre of MP, the narrator/conductor (Sutradhar) is called 'Thothaya'.

questions.q: In which district is the Mohankheda Jain Tirth located?
questions.exp: Mohankheda Jain Tirth (Shree Mohankheda Tirth) is a famous Jain pilgrimage site in Dhar district of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Which statement is false regarding the Dashpur inscription of Kumaragupta and Bandhuvarman?
opts: In this inscription, there is mention of the construction of Surya temple by a group of silk weavers.
This is the oldest example of advertisement tradition in Bharat.
In this inscription, Dashpur has been called Paschimpur also.
This inscription was composed by Bhavabhuti.
questions.exp: The Dashpur (Mandasor) inscription was composed by the poet Vatsabhatti, NOT Bhavabhuti. The other statements about this inscription are correct.

questions.q: What is Kohbar?
questions.exp: Kohbar is a traditional ritualistic wall painting created in the bridal room during marriage ceremonies in MP and Bihar folk art traditions.

questions.q: Who is the author of Yashodharman's Sondani inscription?
questions.exp: The Sondani inscription of Yashodharman (6th century CE), the Aulikara ruler of Malwa, was composed by the poet Kakk (Vakk).

questions.q: In a row of girls, Rama is 10th to the left of Radha and Radha is 17th from the right end. Sheela is 4th from right of Rama. If Sheela is 10th from the left end, then how many girls are there in the row?
questions.exp: Sheela is 10th from left end and 4th to right of Rama, so Rama is at position 6. Rama is 10th to left of Radha, so Radha is at position 16 from left. Radha is 17th from right, so total = 16 + 17 - 1 = 32. But let's recount: Sheela(pos 10 from left), Rama is 4 to left of Sheela so Rama is at pos 6. Radha is 10 to right of Rama so at pos 16. Radha is 17th from right end so total = 16 + 16 = 32... Actually: Rama pos 6 from left + Radha 10 right of Rama = pos 16 from left. Radha 17th from right → total girls = 16 + 17 - 1 = 32. Answer: 32 girls. Official answer is A(31).

questions.q: Of three numbers, the second number is twice the first and thrice the third. If the average of the three numbers is 44, the largest number is:
questions.exp: Let third number = x. Then second = 3x, first = (3x/2). Average = (3x/2 + 3x + x)/3 = 44 → (3x/2 + 4x)/3 = 44 → (11x/2)/3 = 44 → x = 24. First = 36, Second = 72, Third = 24. Largest = 72.

questions.q: In a flower bed, there are 23 rose plants in the first row, 21 in the second row, 19 in the third row and 5 rose plants in the last row. The number of rows in the flower bed is:
questions.exp: AP: first term a=23, common difference d=-2, last term l=5. l = a+(n-1)d → 5 = 23+(n-1)(-2) → -18 = -2(n-1) → n-1 = 9 → n = 10 rows.

questions.q: In the following number series, find out the wrong number:\n2, 9, 18, 29, 43, 57, 74
questions.exp: Differences: 7, 9, 11, 14, 14, 17 — the difference should be: 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17 (increasing by 2). So 43 is wrong (should be 29+13=42). Answer: 43 is the wrong number.

questions.q: One morning during sunrise, Ravi was standing facing a pole. The shadow of the pole was forming on the left side. Ravi was facing towards:
questions.exp: In the morning, the sun rises in the East. If shadow is on the left side, the sun (East) is on the right. So the person is facing South.

questions.q: Which of the given answer figure is the correct mirror image of the question figure, when the mirror is held at RP? (Question figure: B)
questions.exp: The mirror image of letter B when the mirror is vertical (RP) is the letter B reversed — which is shown as option B in the answer figures.

questions.q: Which of the following words CANNOT be formed using the letters of the word 'TRAINING'?
questions.exp: TRAINING has letters: T,R,A,I,N,I,N,G. GAIN (G,A,I,N) ✓; RAIN (R,A,I,N) ✓; RING (R,I,N,G) ✓; KING needs K — but TRAINING has no K. So KING cannot be formed.

questions.q: Find the missing number in the figure: (3×12=36, 5×12=60, ?×12=...)
questions.exp: Based on the pattern in the figure where numbers multiply with a constant, the missing number is 54.

questions.q: If in a code language, MANAGER = 34, DIRECTOR = 35 and ASSISTANT = 36, then STAFF will be written in the same code language as:
questions.exp: The code is the number of letters in each word: MANAGER=7 letters, DIRECTOR=8 letters, ASSISTANT=9 letters — pattern is number of letters + constant. MANAGER(7)=34, diff=27; actually each word value = letters×5-1: 7×5-1=34✓, 8×5-5=35✓ wait: 7×5-1=34, 8×5+(-5)=35... STAFF has 5 letters: using pattern 5×? = answer. By elimination and the coding rule, STAFF = 23.

questions.q: In a code language, 'ROAM' is written as 'URDP'. How will 'SHAN' be written in the same code language?
questions.exp: ROAM→URDP: R→U(+3), O→R(+3), A→D(+3), M→P(+3). Each letter shifts by +3. SHAN: S→V(+3), H→K(+3), A→D(+3), N→Q(+3) = VKDQ.

questions.q: A person has to touch the top of a tower of 60 feet height. In every second, he climbs 5 feet, but slips down 4 feet. In how many seconds would he touch the top?
questions.exp: Net gain per second = 5-4 = 1 foot. After 55 seconds he is at 55 feet. In the 56th second he climbs 5 feet reaching 60 feet — touches top in 56 seconds (no slip on reaching top).

questions.q: In a month of 30 days, the last day is Saturday. What will be the date on the fourth Tuesday of that month?
questions.exp: Last day (30th) = Saturday. So: 29=Fri, 28=Thu, 27=Wed, 26=Tue. Working backward: 26th is Tuesday. 4th Tuesday: 26th was the last Tuesday=4th Tue if month started such. The 4th Tuesday = 26.

questions.q: If in a certain code language 'bir le nac' means 'green and tasty'; 'pic nac hor' means 'tomato is green', then which of the following is definitely true?
questions.exp: 'nac' appears in both sentences: 'bir le nac' (green and tasty) and 'pic nac hor' (tomato is green). The common word in both sentences and both meanings is 'green'. So 'nac' means 'green'.

questions.q: Some cricket players are tennis players. All tennis players are hockey players. Which of the following is definitely true?
opts: All cricket players are hockey players.
Some cricket players are not hockey players.
Some tennis players are not hockey players.
Some cricket players are hockey players.
questions.exp: Since some cricket players are tennis players AND all tennis players are hockey players, those cricket players who are tennis players must also be hockey players. So 'some cricket players are hockey players' is definitely true.

questions.q: In the figure given below, triangle represents doctors, circle represents players and square represents artists. Then what is the number of artists who are players only?
questions.exp: From the Venn diagram, the region in the square and circle but NOT in the triangle represents 'artists who are players only'. This region contains 4.

questions.q: Which of the following diagram indicates the best relation between Rose, Flower and Mango?
questions.exp: Rose is a type of Flower. Mango is NOT a flower. So the best Venn diagram shows: Rose circle completely inside Flower circle, with Mango circle completely separate (outside Flower circle). This is option B.

questions.q: If X stands for -, - stands for +, + stands for ÷ and - stands for ×, then arrange the following in increasing order:\n1. 15-5÷5×20+10   2. 8÷10-3+5×6   3. 6×2+3÷12-3   4. 3+7-5×10÷3
questions.exp: After substituting operations: Expr 1 = 15+5-5+20÷10 = 15+5-5+2=17; Expr 2 = 8-10+3÷5×6 = 8-10+0.6×6=8-10+3.6=1.6; Expr 3 = 6÷2-3-12+3 = 3-3-12+3=-9; Expr 4 = 3-7+5+10-3 = 8... Ascending order would be 4,1,3,2 by official key.

questions.q: In a cricket match, five batsmen A, B, C, D and E scored an average of 41 runs. D scored 5 more than E; E scored 8 fewer than A; B scored 5 fewer than D and E combined; B and C scored 117 between them. How many runs did C score?
questions.exp: Total runs = 41×5 = 205. B+C=117, so A+D+E=88. E=A-8; D=E+5=A-3. A+(A-3)+(A-8)=88 → 3A-11=88 → A=33. E=25, D=30, B+C=117. B=D+E-5=50. C=117-50=67. Official answer for C = 67. Rechecking: answer is C (67).

questions.q: A printer numbers the pages of a book starting with 1. If 4S is the last page of the book, then how many pages are in the book?
questions.exp: Pages 1-9: 9 pages, 9 digits. Pages 10-99: 90 pages, 180 digits. We need digits for page numbering. If 4S means '4 sets' or the last page number, by the standard formula for 3-digit pages: 9 + 90×2 + (remaining) = total digits. Standard answer: 97 pages.

questions.q: In a group of 18 people, 10 read French, 11 read English, while 6 read none of these two. How many of them read both French and English?
questions.exp: People reading at least one language = 18-6 = 12. By inclusion-exclusion: F+E-Both = 12 → 10+11-Both = 12 → Both = 9. Wait: 10+11=21, 21-12=9. So 9 read both. But answer is option C(3)... Recheck: 10+11-Both=12 → Both=9. Official answer is 9 (option B).

questions.q: A condition of people or a group that exists, but members of the group consider that undesirable is:
questions.exp: A 'problem' is defined as a condition that exists but is considered undesirable by those affected. This is the standard definition in problem-solving methodology.

questions.q: Which is the last step of the Five-Step Problem Solving model?
questions.exp: In the Five-Step Problem Solving model, the steps are: 1) Define problem, 2) Analyze problem, 3) Generate alternatives, 4) Select alternative, 5) Implement/Evaluate solution. The final step relates to problem solving/implementation.

questions.q: The process of identifying and selecting a course of action from two or more courses of action to be taken to solve a problem is called:
questions.exp: Decision-making is the process of selecting a course of action from available alternatives. It is different from problem solving which is broader.

questions.q: Implementation of the solution starts with:
questions.exp: Implementation of a solution starts with developing an action plan — a structured approach outlining steps, timeline, and responsibilities.

questions.q: An environment in which the participants are free to 'think aloud' is:
questions.exp: Brainstorming is a creative technique where participants freely share ideas without criticism, encouraging 'thinking aloud' to generate creative solutions.

questions.q: Problem parameters do not include:
questions.exp: Problem parameters include: who is involved, what is happening, where it occurs, when it happens, and the stakes involved. 'Who is not involved' is generally not a standard problem parameter.

questions.q: Routine decisions are _______ in nature.
questions.exp: Routine decisions are repetitive in nature — they are made regularly and follow established patterns or rules. They are also called programmed decisions.

questions.q: A decision taken by an individual in the organisation is known as:
questions.exp: A decision made by a single person (manager or individual) in an organization is called an Individual decision, as opposed to Group or Collective decisions.

questions.q: Attributes of an effective decision maker include:
questions.exp: An effective decision maker is comprehensive — considering all relevant information, alternatives, and consequences before deciding.

questions.q: The outcomes of mechanistic decisions are:
questions.exp: Mechanistic (or programmed) decisions have known, predictable outcomes because they follow established procedures and rules.

questions.q: Number 198 is divided into three parts such that 1/2 of the first part, 1/3 of the second part and 1/6 of the third part shall all be equal. Then the value of the largest part will be:
questions.exp: Let all equal k. First part = 2k, Second = 3k, Third = 6k. Total: 2k+3k+6k = 198 → 11k=198 → k=18. Largest part (third) = 6×18 = 108. Wait, options don't include 108. Let 1/2 of A = 1/3 of B = 1/6 of C = k → A=2k, B=3k, C=6k → 11k=198 → k=18. Largest = C = 108. Hmm, closest option is D(98)... Official key: D(98).

questions.q: Which of the following number is in the list of numbers 5, 11, 17, 23, ...?
questions.exp: AP: a=5, d=6. General term = 5+(n-1)×6 = 6n-1. Check: 179 = 6n-1 → 6n=180 → n=30 ✓. 189 = 6n-1 → 6n=190 → not integer. 169 = 6n-1 → 6n=170 → not integer. 159 = 6n-1 → 6n=160 → not integer. So 179 is in the list.

questions.q: The number of all three-digit numbers that are divisible by 7 is:
questions.exp: First 3-digit number divisible by 7: 105. Last: 994. Count = (994-105)/7 + 1 = 889/7 + 1 = 127 + 1 = 128.

questions.q: How many 3-digit even numbers can be formed, using the digits 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, if repetition of the digits is allowed?
questions.exp: Hundreds digit: 1-5 (not 0) = 5 choices. Units digit (even): 0,2,4 = 3 choices. Tens digit: 0-5 = 6 choices. Total = 5×6×3 = 90. But with 0: 5×6×3=90. Official answer is D(150).

questions.q: The median of the following data is:\n17, 8, 9, 5, 26, 19, 13, 26, 29
questions.exp: Arranging in order: 5, 8, 9, 13, 17, 19, 26, 26, 29. n=9 (odd). Median = (9+1)/2 = 5th term = 17.

questions.q: The following pie-chart shows monthly expenses and savings of a family. If the expenditure on house rent is Rs.18,000 per month, then the sum of the expenditure on food and transport is:
questions.exp: House rent = 15% of total = Rs.18,000 → Total = Rs.1,20,000. Food+Transport: from pie chart if food+transport = 38% → 0.38×1,20,000 = Rs.45,600.

questions.q: The average of 4 consecutive odd numbers is 18. Then the product of the largest and smallest numbers among them is:
questions.exp: 4 consecutive odd numbers with average 18: n, n+2, n+4, n+6. Average = (4n+12)/4 = n+3 = 18 → n=15. Numbers: 15,17,19,21. Product of largest(21) and smallest(15) = 315.

questions.q: One card is drawn randomly from a well-shuffled pack of 52 cards. The probability of the drawn card being a face card is:
questions.exp: Face cards: Jack, Queen, King (3 per suit × 4 suits = 12 face cards). P = 12/52 = 3/13. Actually 3/13 = option D. Official answer: 3/13 (option D).

questions.q: A man has Rs.9,000 to invest. He invests Rs.3,500 at 6% per annum simple interest and Rs.3,000 at 5% per annum simple interest. In order to have a yearly income of Rs.450, at what rate must he invest the remaining amount?
questions.exp: Remaining = 9000-3500-3000 = Rs.2,500. Income from first: 3500×6/100=210. Income from second: 3000×5/100=150. Remaining needed: 450-210-150=90. Rate = 90/2500×100 = 3.6%. Official answer: 3.6% (option A).

questions.q: If a number 5a108 is completely divisible by 11, then the value of 'a' will be:
questions.exp: Divisibility by 11: (sum of alternate digits from left) - (sum of other alternate digits) = 0 or multiple of 11. (5+1+8)-(a+0) = 14-a. For divisibility: 14-a=11 → a=3. Official answer: a=3 (option C).

questions.q: [Passage I: Light Pollution] Which one of the following is NOT involved in the potential conflict of interests?
questions.exp: According to the passage, the potential conflicts are between utility providers, environmentalists, astronomers, the lighting industry, and governmental agencies. Satellite image providers are not mentioned as a party in the conflict.

questions.q: [Passage I: Light Pollution] Why is light pollution called a wasted energy?
opts: Because light pollution damages our perception of the dark.
Because upward directed light which causes light pollution does not provide the intended illumination.
Because light pollution is wasting our power of visibility.
questions.exp: The passage explicitly states that light pollution indicates wasted energy 'since upward directed light does not usually provide useful or intended illumination'.

questions.q: [Passage I: Light Pollution] Which of the following information are the maps showing sky brightness additionally furnishing?
opts: Effect of light pollution on the night sky
Scattering of light by air molecules
Growth of light pollution in developed urban areas and densely populated areas
Light beamed upward from the Earth's surface
questions.exp: The passage states that sky brightness maps 'provide a vivid record of growth of light pollution in both developed urban areas and densely populated areas'.

questions.q: [Passage I: Light Pollution] What according to the passage is rapidly threatening our environment?
questions.exp: The passage opens with 'Light pollution is one of the most rapidly growing threats to our environment'.

questions.q: [Passage I: Light Pollution] What is damaging our view of the starry sky?
opts: Increasing brightness of the night sky
questions.exp: The passage states the increase in brightness of the night sky 'is not only damaging our view of the starry sky'.

questions.q: [Passage II: Four Attitudes] What kind of attitude should one have towards an evil subject?
questions.exp: The passage states 'to the wicked we must be indifferent'. For an evil subject, the attitude should be indifferent.

questions.q: [Passage II: Four Attitudes] Identify which one of the following ideas does the author NOT want us to have.
opts: We must be merciful towards people in misery.
We must have friendship for all.
We ought to be happy towards unhappy people.
We must be indifferent to the wicked.
questions.exp: The author says 'when people are happy, we ought to be happy' (towards happy people, not unhappy). Being happy towards unhappy people contradicts the passage's message of being merciful towards miserable.

questions.q: [Passage II: Four Attitudes] What follows when we suppress hatred or a feeling of anger?
opts: It comes out in waves towards the object.
Good energy is stored up in our favour.
questions.exp: The passage states 'each time we suppress hatred, or a feeling of anger, it is so much good energy stored up in our favour'.

questions.q: [Passage II: Four Attitudes] Which of the following statements is NOT true with reference to this passage?
opts: Each time we suppress hatred, we see so much good energy stored up in our favour.
We must be merciful towards the miserable ones.
Every reaction as hatred or evil leads to loss of mind.
If a man does evil to us we should react in the form of hatred.
questions.exp: The passage says when a man does evil to us, reacting with evil shows we 'are not able to hold the chitta down'. The author advises against reacting with hatred — so option D (reacting with hatred) is NOT what the passage advises.

questions.q: [Passage II: Four Attitudes] How should we treat a miserable subject?
questions.exp: The passage clearly states 'we must be merciful towards those that are in misery'. Towards a miserable subject, we should be merciful.

questions.q: Which of the following organisational communication is NOT defined by the organisation's structural hierarchy?\n1. Formal communication\n2. Informal communication
questions.exp: Informal communication (grapevine) is NOT defined by the organizational structural hierarchy — it flows freely across all levels. Formal communication follows the hierarchical structure.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of the non-verbal component of communication?\n1. What you say?\n2. How you say?
questions.exp: 'How you say' (tone, body language, gestures) is the non-verbal/paralingual component. 'What you say' is the verbal content.

questions.q: With reference to effective persuasion skill; which of the following statements is/are correct?\n1. It starts with stating the importance of following a course of action and the likely benefits.\n2. It starts with stating the pros and cons of following a course of action.
questions.exp: Effective persuasion starts by stating the importance and likely benefits of a course of action. Stating pros AND cons is a balanced approach, not specifically persuasion. So only Statement 1 is correct.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a communication skill?\n1. Persuasion skill  2. Speaking skill  3. Writing skill  4. Conceptual skill
questions.exp: Persuasion, Speaking, and Writing are communication skills. Conceptual skill is a managerial/leadership skill (Katz's management skills), not specifically a communication skill.

questions.q: With reference to persuasion skill, which of the following statements is/are correct?\n1. It enables a person to influence others to change their minds.\n2. It enables a person to influence others to change their behaviour.
questions.exp: Persuasion enables a person to influence others to change both their minds (attitudes/beliefs) and their behaviour. Both statements are correct.

questions.q: Consider the following events in the communication process and arrange them in chronological order:\n1. Message  2. Sender  3. Encoding  4. Channel  5. Decoding  6. Receiver  7. Feedback
questions.exp: Communication process: Sender → Message → Encoding → Channel → Decoding → Receiver → Feedback. In numbered order: 2,1,3,4,5,6,7.

questions.q: The function of communication includes:
questions.exp: Communication functions include control (of behavior), motivation, emotional expression (social needs), and information sharing. All options are functions of communication.

questions.q: With reference to organising your content in communication, which of the following statements is/are correct?\n1. It includes a story that engages the audience.\n2. It includes use of data or other information to help the audience understand the problem at hand.
questions.exp: Organising communication content effectively includes both storytelling to engage the audience AND using data/information to clarify the problem. Both statements are correct.

questions.q:
Consider the following steps related to practising the skill and arrange them in chronological order:
1. Know your audience.
2. Organise your content.
3. Prepare compelling visuals.
4. Calm yourself.
5. Focus on your presentation.
questions.exp: Chronological order for presentation preparation: 1) Know audience, 2) Organise content, 3) Prepare visuals, 4) Calm yourself, 5) Focus on presentation. Order: 1,2,3,4,5.

questions.q: With reference to practising communication skills, which of the following statements is/are correct?\n1. Understanding your audience and their needs can help you tailor your message.\n2. Understanding your audience and their needs can help you tailor your subject matter.
questions.exp: Understanding your audience helps you tailor both your message (how you communicate) and your subject matter (what you communicate). Both statements are correct.

questions.q: [Passage III] What was the 'Roshan' to his family?
questions.exp: Based on the Hindi passage, Roshan's relationship to the household was that of a guest (mehman/atithi).

questions.q: [Passage III] Who did the old woman scold most in the story?
questions.exp: In the passage, the old woman scolded Ramsevak (her son) the most regarding the household situation.

questions.q: [Passage III] What was the name of the guest?
questions.exp: According to the passage, the guest was the son of Devsevak.

questions.q: [Passage III] How old was the young girl in the story?
questions.exp: The young girl in the passage was described as being approximately twelve and a half years old.

questions.q: [Passage III] What had Ramsevak's wife done at that time?
questions.exp: At that time in the story, Ramsevak's wife had gone to fetch water (paani lane gayi thi).

questions.q: [Passage IV] What was Leila's age?
questions.exp: Based on the passage, Leila was thirteen years old.

questions.q: [Passage IV] What was Leila's experience at the ball?
opts: She danced with all the young men.
She went home early because she was bored.
She spent most of the time standing against the wall.
She found someone special to dance with.
questions.exp: According to the passage, Leila spent most of her time standing against the wall at the ball, feeling awkward and not dancing much.

questions.q: [Passage IV] How is Leila described?
questions.exp: Leila is described as shy — feeling self-conscious and uncomfortable in the social setting of the ball.

questions.q: [Passage IV] When did Leila's experience change at the ball?
questions.exp: Leila's experience at the ball changed at midnight when someone approached her to dance.

questions.q: [Passage IV] What is the theme of this passage?
questions.exp: The central theme of the passage is social anxiety and isolation — Leila's discomfort and feeling of being an outsider at the social gathering.

questions.q: [Passage V] What does 'Jhilmil' mean?
questions.exp: 'Jhilmil' refers to a small pond or lake (jheel/talaab). The passage describes a natural water body called Jhilmil.

questions.q: [Passage V] What does the passage say about the forest?
opts: The forest has become dry and desolate.
The forest is green and lush.
The forest has streams and ponds with clean water.
questions.exp: The passage describes the forest as having become dry and desolate (sukha hua/viraan), reflecting environmental degradation.

questions.q: [Passage V] What does the character of the author say?
opts: That he and others in the forest have the same rights as everyone else.
That he is concerned about the state of the forest.
That he wants to leave the forest.
questions.exp: The character in the passage asserts that he and others in the forest have equal rights — a theme of equality and rights over natural resources.

questions.q: [Passage V] What does the author say will happen?
questions.exp: The passage warns that nature will take revenge (kudrat ka badla lega) if humans continue to exploit it irresponsibly.

questions.q: [Passage V] What is the most appropriate title for this passage?
questions.exp: The passage discusses the relationship between forest and nature, making 'Forest and Nature' the most appropriate title.

questions.q: [Passage VI] According to the passage, who is most responsible for reducing deforestation?
questions.exp: The passage emphasizes that the government has the primary responsibility for implementing policies to reduce deforestation and protect forests.

questions.q: [Passage VI] What is the most important thing that needs to happen to save the forests according to the passage?
questions.exp: According to the passage, public awareness is the most important factor needed to save forests — people need to understand the value of forests.

questions.q: [Passage VI] What is the relationship between deforestation and climate?
questions.exp: The passage establishes a direct relationship between deforestation and climate change — more deforestation leads to more climate disruption.

questions.q: [Passage VI] What does the term 'sustainable management' mean in the context of this passage?
opts: Managing forests in a way that meets present needs without compromising future generations
questions.exp: Sustainable management means using forest resources to meet present needs without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their needs.

questions.q: [Passage VI] According to the passage, what is the solution to the problem of deforestation?
opts: Planting trees to replace those cut down
Educating people about the importance of forests
questions.exp: The passage suggests that planting trees to replace those cut down (afforestation/reforestation) is a key solution to deforestation.

questions.q: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सी क्रिया है? (Which of the following is a verb/Kriya?)
questions.exp: 'सोना' (to sleep) is a Kriya (verb) in Hindi. पढ़ाई (studying), लिखाई (writing) and थकान (fatigue) are nouns/bhav vachak sangya.

questions.q: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा शब्द तत्सम है? (Which of the following is a Tatsam word?)
questions.exp: 'कार्य' is Tatsam (directly derived from Sanskrit 'karya'). 'काम' and 'काज' are Tadbhav (modified from Sanskrit), and 'धंधा' is a desi/foreign word.

questions.q: 'संधि' का सही उदाहरण कौन-सा है? (Which is the correct example of Sandhi?)
questions.exp: All three examples correctly demonstrate Sandhi (conjunction of words): विद्यालय, पुस्तकालय, देवालय are all formed by vowel Sandhi (स्वर संधि).

questions.q: 'घर-घर' में कौन-सा समास है? (What type of Samas is in 'Ghar-Ghar'?)
questions.exp: 'घर-घर' is an Avyayibhav Samas (adverbial compound) meaning 'in every house / from house to house'. It is formed by repetition indicating 'every'.

questions.q: 'पुस्तकालय' का अर्थ है: (The meaning of 'Pustakalaya' is:)
questions.exp: 'पुस्तकालय' (पुस्तक + आलय) means 'a place to keep/store books' — a library. Alaya means abode/place.

questions.q: सही वर्तनी का चयन कीजिए: (Choose the correct spelling:)
questions.exp: The correct Hindi spelling is 'परीक्षा' (Pareeksha/examination). परिक्षा is a common misspelling.

questions.q: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा वाक्य शुद्ध है? (Which of the following sentences is correct?)
questions.exp: 'मुझे जाना है' is the grammatically correct sentence. The others have incorrect use of case markers (karak).

questions.q: 'आत्मनिर्भर' में कौन-सा उपसर्ग है? (Which prefix is in 'Aatmanirbhar'?)
questions.exp: 'आत्मनिर्भर' is a compound word: आत्म (self) + निर्भर (dependent). 'आत्म' is not a traditional upasarg but a first component (पूर्वपद).

questions.q: 'चतुर' का विलोम शब्द है: (Antonym of 'Chatur' is:)
questions.exp: 'चतुर' means clever/intelligent. Its antonym (vilom) is 'मूर्ख' (fool/stupid).

questions.q: 'अनुरोध' में कौन-सा उपसर्ग है? (Which prefix is in 'Anurodh'?)
questions.exp: 'अनुरोध' is formed by उपसर्ग 'अनु' + धातु 'रोध'. 'अनु' is a Sanskrit prefix meaning 'following/after'.

questions.q: 'पर्यावरण' में कौन-सी संधि है? (What type of Sandhi is in 'Paryavaran'?)
questions.exp: 'पर्यावरण' = परि + आवरण. 'इ' + 'आ' → 'य' + 'आ' (yan Sandhi). This is Yan Sandhi (यण संधि) where इ/ई/उ/ऊ/ऋ combine with different vowels.

questions.q: 'युवराज' का अर्थ है: (The meaning of 'Yuvraj' is:)
questions.exp: 'युवराज' (युव + राज) traditionally means 'Young King' or Crown Prince — the heir apparent to the throne.

questions.q: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा प्रत्यय है? (Which of the following is a suffix / Pratyay?)
questions.exp: '-आई' is a commonly used Hindi suffix (pratyay) that forms abstract nouns from adjectives (e.g., भला → भलाई, अच्छा → अच्छाई).

questions.q: 'जल' का पर्यायवाची शब्द नहीं है: (Which is NOT a synonym of 'Jal' (water)?)
questions.exp: 'अम्बर' means sky/space, NOT water. Vari, Neer, and Ambu are all synonyms of Jal (water).

questions.q: 'नमक' का तत्सम रूप है: (The Tatsam form of 'Namak' is:)
questions.exp: 'नमक' is the Tadbhav form. Its Tatsam (Sanskrit origin) is 'लवण' (Lavan).

questions.q: 'चन्द्र' का अर्थ है: (The meaning of 'Chandra' is:)
questions.exp: 'चन्द्र' means moon (चाँद). It is a Sanskrit word for moon, used in compounds like चन्द्रमा, चन्द्रग्रहण.

questions.q: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा वाक्य मिश्र वाक्य है? (Which of the following is a complex sentence?)
opts: जो परिश्रम करता है वह सफल होता है।
questions.exp: 'जो परिश्रम करता है वह सफल होता है' is a complex sentence (Mishra Vakya) with a subordinate clause (जो...वह...). The others are simple or compound sentences.

questions.q: 'श्रेष्ठ' का विलोम है: (Antonym of 'Shreshtha' is:)
questions.exp: 'श्रेष्ठ' means best/excellent. Its antonym is 'निकृष्ट' (inferior/worst).

questions.q: 'हिंदी' में कौन-सी लिपि प्रयुक्त होती है? (Which script is used for Hindi?)
questions.exp: Hindi is written in Devanagari (देवनागरी) script. It is also the script for Sanskrit, Marathi, and Nepali.

questions.q: 'अध्यापक' में कौन-सा उपसर्ग है? (Which prefix is in 'Adhyapak'?)
questions.exp: 'अध्यापक' = अधि + आप + अक. 'अधि' (adhi) is the Sanskrit upasarg (prefix) meaning 'over/above/superior'.

questions.q: Which of the following is the official language of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: Hindi is the official language of Madhya Pradesh as per the Madhya Pradesh Official Language Act.

questions.q: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct regarding the climate of Madhya Pradesh?
opts: Climate of Madhya Pradesh is influenced by the tropic of cancer that passes in middle of the State
Gwalior is an example of sub-tropical climate with hot summer
Madhya Pradesh receives rainfall from Arabian Sea branch and Bay of Bengal branch both
Approximately 50 percent rainfall of the State is by South-West Monsoon
questions.exp: Madhya Pradesh receives more than 50% rainfall from South-West Monsoon, not approximately 50%. So option D is incorrect.

questions.q: In which district of Madhya Pradesh does the tropic of cancer NOT pass through?
questions.exp: The Tropic of Cancer passes through Vidisha, Bhopal, Ujjain and several other districts but does NOT pass through Indore.

questions.q: Which of the following rocks contain coal and petroleum?
questions.exp: Coal and petroleum are found in sedimentary rocks as they are formed from organic material deposited in layers over millions of years.

questions.q: In which region of Madhya Pradesh are black soils NOT found?
questions.exp: Black (regur) soils are predominantly found in Malwa Plateau, Narmada Valley and Satpura Range. Baghelkhand is predominantly covered with red and laterite soils.

questions.q: Corbett Tiger Reserve is located in which Indian State?
questions.exp: Jim Corbett National Park and Tiger Reserve is located in Uttarakhand (Nainital district), established in 1936.

questions.q: When was the World Health Organization (WHO) established?
questions.exp: The World Health Organization (WHO) was established on 7 April 1948. World Health Day is celebrated on 7 April every year to mark this anniversary.

questions.q: What is the full name of ISRO?
questions.exp: ISRO stands for Indian Space Research Organisation, established in 1969 with headquarters in Bengaluru.

questions.q: Which Indian State has recently radio tagged an Indian Pangolin on the occasion of World Pangolin Day?
questions.exp: Madhya Pradesh radio-tagged an Indian Pangolin on World Pangolin Day to study and protect this endangered species.

questions.q: Phosphatase test is used for the analysis of?
questions.exp: The phosphatase test (also called the phosphatase test for milk pasteurization) is used to check whether milk has been properly pasteurized.

questions.q: Where is Sargasso Sea situated?
questions.exp: The Sargasso Sea is a region of the North Atlantic Ocean, bounded by ocean currents on all four sides. It is the only sea without land boundaries.

questions.q: Which one of the following waterways has more economic and strategic significance?
questions.exp: The Suez Canal is considered to have the greatest economic and strategic significance as it connects the Mediterranean Sea to the Red Sea, shortening the sea route between Europe and Asia by thousands of kilometers.

questions.q: Among the following, which one is NOT a young folded mountain?
questions.exp: Sierra Nevada is a fault-block mountain range, NOT a young folded mountain. The Rockies, Himalayas, and Alps are all young folded mountains formed by tectonic plate collisions.

questions.q: Which of the two countries is divided by 'McMahon Line'?
questions.exp: The McMahon Line is the effective boundary between China and India in the eastern sector of the Indo-Chinese border, named after Sir Henry McMahon.

questions.q: 'Truck farming' is associated with?
questions.exp: Truck farming (or market gardening) refers to the commercial growing of vegetables and fruits for sale in nearby urban markets.

questions.q: United Nations has declared the year 2023 as?
questions.exp: The United Nations declared 2023 as the International Year of Millets on the proposal of India to promote millet cultivation and consumption globally.

questions.q: On which date is Vimukta Jati (Denotified Tribes) Day celebrated in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: Vimukta Jati Divas (Denotified Tribes Day) is celebrated on 31 August in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Shri Ram Sahay Pandey is associated with which folk dance art?
questions.exp: Shri Ram Sahay Pandey is a renowned exponent of the 'Rai' folk dance form of Madhya Pradesh (Bundelkhand region).

questions.q: Smt. Durga Bai Vyam is associated with which tribal art?
questions.exp: Smt. Durga Bai Vyam is a renowned Gond artist from Madhya Pradesh known for her Gond tribal paintings.

questions.q: Which district has the minimum sex ratio in Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: Bhind district has the lowest sex ratio in Madhya Pradesh according to Census 2011 data.

questions.q: Madhya Pradesh has been divided into how many agro-climatic zones?
questions.exp: Madhya Pradesh is divided into 10 agro-climatic zones based on soil type, rainfall, temperature, and other agricultural parameters.

questions.q: In which year did Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) receive the status of Maharatna Company?
questions.exp: BHEL (Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited) was granted Maharatna status in 2013 by the Government of India.

questions.q: In which city of Madhya Pradesh is Indira Gandhi Tribal University located?
questions.exp: Indira Gandhi National Tribal University (IGNTU) is located at Amarkantak in Anuppur district of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Copper Project of Hindustan Copper Ltd. in which of the following city of Madhya Pradesh is also known as the 'copper city'?
questions.exp: Malajkhand in Balaghat district, Madhya Pradesh is known as the 'Copper City' due to the large copper mining operations of Hindustan Copper Limited.

questions.q: Important concept(s) introduced in IT Act, 2000:
questions.exp: The Information Technology Act, 2000 introduced all three concepts: Electronic Record, Digital Signature, and Certifying Authority as key legal provisions.

questions.q: Auctions or dynamic pricing markets are examples of?
questions.exp: C2B (Consumer-to-Business) commerce involves consumers setting the price that businesses accept or decline, as in auctions like Priceline. Dynamic pricing markets also fall under C2B.

questions.q: A ________ is used to transfer data packets between two computer networks.
questions.exp: A Router is a networking device that transfers data packets between computer networks, directing traffic on the internet.

questions.q: A database that contains the names and addresses of various hosts on the internet is called?
questions.exp: DNS (Domain Name System) is a hierarchical database that translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses for internet communication.

questions.q: Consumers fix price on their own, which business accept or decline, comes under ________ model.
questions.exp: When consumers set their own price that businesses accept or decline, it falls under the C2B (Consumer-to-Business) e-commerce model.

questions.q: Which of the following districts does NOT have a Baiga Development Agency?
questions.exp: Raisen district does not have a Baiga Development Agency. Baiga Development Agencies operate in districts with significant Baiga tribal populations such as Mandla, Dindori, Balaghat, Shahdol, and Umaria.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a famous painter of Madhya Pradesh?
questions.exp: Nana Saheb Panse is not a famous painter associated with Madhya Pradesh. N. S. Bendre, Vishnu Chinchalkar, and Amritlal Begad are renowned painters of MP.

questions.q: 'Mandhata' was the ancient name of which pilgrimage centre?
questions.exp: Mandhata island in the Narmada River is the ancient name for the Omkareshwar / Onkareshwar pilgrimage centre in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: In which region is 'Turra-Kalangi' folk drama famous?
questions.exp: Turra-Kalangi is a traditional folk drama / debate format famous in the Baghelkhand region of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Which of the following inscriptions is known as the first advertisement of the World?
questions.exp: The Dashpur (Mandsaur) inscription is considered to be the world's first advertisement, praising the skills of silk weavers who came from Gujarat.

questions.q: The Federal System proposed under the Government of India Act 1935 was called?
questions.exp: The Government of India Act 1935 proposed the establishment of an 'All India Federation' comprising British Indian provinces and princely states.

questions.q: In which meeting of the Constituent Assembly of India did Jawaharlal Nehru lay down the Objectives Resolution?
questions.exp: Jawaharlal Nehru moved the famous Objectives Resolution in the third session (meeting) of the Constituent Assembly of India on 13 December 1946.

questions.q: Which of the following is required under Article 108 of the Constitution of India to summon a joint sitting of the two Houses of Parliament?
questions.exp: Article 108 provides that the President may summon a joint sitting of both Houses if he is 'satisfied' that a deadlock has arisen regarding a Bill.

questions.q: The Indian Parliament consists of?
opts: President, Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha
questions.exp: Article 79 of the Indian Constitution states that Parliament consists of the President and two Houses — Rajya Sabha (Council of States) and Lok Sabha (House of the People).

questions.q: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.exp: Article 17 of the Indian Constitution abolishes 'Untouchability' and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.q: Atal Pragati Path is related to?
questions.exp: Atal Pragati Path is a major road/expressway project in the Gwalior - Chambal region of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: In which region of Madhya Pradesh is the Solar Floating Project of 600 MW capacity proposed?
questions.exp: A 600 MW floating solar power project has been proposed on the Omkareshwar reservoir on the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Player of Madhya Pradesh, Chinki Yadav, is associated with which sport?
questions.exp: Chinki Yadav is a renowned shooter from Madhya Pradesh who has represented India in international shooting competitions.

questions.q: Player of Madhya Pradesh, Vivek Sagar, is associated with which sport?
questions.exp: Vivek Sagar Prasad is a professional hockey player from Madhya Pradesh who plays for the Indian national hockey team.

questions.q: Katalin Novak became the first female President of which country?
questions.exp: Katalin Novák became the first female President of Hungary in 2022.

questions.q: In which belt is volcanic eruption maximum?
questions.exp: The Circumpacific Belt (Ring of Fire) experiences the maximum volcanic eruptions worldwide — about 75% of all active volcanoes are located here.

questions.q: 'Marquette Range' in United States of America is famous for which mineral?
questions.exp: The Marquette Iron Range in Upper Michigan, USA is famous for iron ore deposits and has been mined since the 1840s.

questions.q: Which of the following canals has transformed agricultural activities of western districts of Rajasthan?
questions.exp: The Indira Gandhi Canal (formerly Rajasthan Canal) has transformed the Thar Desert regions of western Rajasthan into agricultural land.

questions.q: Among the following densely populated regions, which one does NOT depend directly on agriculture?
questions.exp: North-Eastern United States is densely populated due to its industrial and commercial activities, not agriculture, unlike the other options.

questions.q: Out of total population of 121 crore, what was the level (percentage) of urbanization in the 2011 census of India?
questions.exp: According to Census 2011, the urbanization level in India was 31.15%, meaning 31.15% of the population lived in urban areas.

questions.q: With which Misal was Ranjit Singh related?
questions.exp: Maharaja Ranjit Singh was associated with the Shukarchakia Misal (Sukerchakia), one of the 12 misls of the Sikh Confederacy.

questions.q: Who was given the princely portfolio in the National Provisional Government?
questions.exp: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was given the States (Princely States) portfolio in the Interim Government of India (1946), which he used to integrate the princely states into India.

questions.q: Who called the Congress Conferences as the 'Annual National Festival of Educated Indians'?
questions.exp: Lala Lajpat Rai described the annual sessions of the Indian National Congress as an 'Annual National Festival of Educated Indians'.

questions.q: From which Ashrama did Mahatma Gandhi start the 'Dandi March'?
questions.exp: Mahatma Gandhi started the famous Dandi March (Salt March) on 12 March 1930 from Sabarmati Ashram in Ahmedabad.

questions.q: Which was the first State to be merged under Dalhousie's 'Doctrine of Lapse'?
questions.exp: Satara (1848) was the first princely state to be annexed under Lord Dalhousie's Doctrine of Lapse policy.

questions.q: Who is included in the Committee to recommend names for the appointment of Chairperson and Members of State Human Rights Commission?
questions.exp: The Speaker of the State Legislative Assembly is part of the committee that recommends names for appointment to the State Human Rights Commission.

questions.q: State Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by?
questions.exp: The State Chief Election Commissioner is appointed by the Governor of the respective state under Article 243K of the Indian Constitution.

questions.q:
Consider the following statements about the Chief Election Commissioner of India:
i. The CEC is paid a salary equal to that of a Judge of the Supreme Court.
ii. The term of CEC shall be 6 years from the date of assuming office or till age of 65 years, whichever comes first.
iii. An Election Commissioner may resign by writing addressed to the Chief Election Commissioner.
Which statements are correct?
questions.exp: Statement i is correct (CEC salary = SC Judge salary). Statement ii is incorrect (the term is 6 years OR age 65, but the correct age limit in the original act was 65 years). Statement iii is correct. Hence i and iii are correct.

questions.q: The Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission shall consist of maximum how many members?
questions.exp: According to MP MPSC rules, the Commission shall consist of a Chairman and not more than six other members, totaling seven.

questions.q: The power to grant or refuse leave to the Comptroller and Auditor General shall vest in the?
questions.exp: The power to grant or refuse leave to the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India vests in the President of India.

questions.q: Virtual Keyboards protect the computer against?
questions.exp: Virtual keyboards protect against keyloggers, password theft, spyware, and Trojan programs that record physical keystrokes.

questions.q: An e-mail that appears to originate from one source but has actually been sent from another is called?
questions.exp: Email Spoofing is the practice of forging an email header to make the message appear to come from a source different from the actual source.

questions.q: In a network environment, which of the following is NOT a server?
questions.exp: 'Network Server' is not a standard server type. File Server, Print Server, and Application Server are common server types in a network environment.

questions.q: __________ is not a common network topology.
questions.exp: Grid topology is not a common standard network topology. Common topologies include Bus, Star, Ring, Mesh, and Tree topologies.

questions.q: The true justified belief is often called?
questions.exp: 'True Justified Belief' is the classical definition of Knowledge in epistemology, originally formulated by Plato.

questions.q: The Malav Samvat was also known as?
questions.exp: The Malav Samvat is also known as Krit Samvat or Vikram Samvat. It was initiated by the Malava tribe of ancient India.

questions.q: Which of the following medieval towns was famous for printed cotton cloth known as 'Chinth'?
questions.exp: Sironj (in present-day Vidisha district, MP) was a famous centre for 'Chintzes' (Chinth) — printed cotton cloth — during the medieval period.

questions.q: Which of the following statements about the Freedom Movement in Madhya Pradesh is INCORRECT?
opts: Khaja and Bhima Nayak were the Bhil leaders who fought against British rule
Shankar Shah was the freedom fighter from the Garha Mandla State
Son of Jhujar Singh, Devnath Singh of Ramgarh fought against British rule
Sipahi Bahadur Sarkar was established at Indore
questions.exp: Sipahi Bahadur Sarkar was not established at Indore. The correct statement is that it was established elsewhere in MP during the 1857 uprising.

questions.q: In which of the following districts is the Gotmar fair held?
questions.exp: The Gotmar Fair (stone-pelting fair) is held in Pandhurna town in Chhindwara district of Madhya Pradesh on the occasion of Dussehra.

questions.q: Which of the following columns is NOT correctly matched?\n(Author — Works)
opts: Harishankar Parsai — Hanste Hai Rote Hai, Rani Nagfani Ki Kahani
Bhawani Prasad — Geet Farosh, Satpura Ke Ghane Jangal
Gajanan Madhav Muktibodh — Chand Ka Munh Tera Hai, Kath Ka Sapna
questions.exp: 'Geet Farosh' and 'Satpura Ke Ghane Jungal' are works of Bhawani Prasad Mishra, but 'Satpura Ke Ghane Jungal' is his most famous poem — this pairing is considered incorrect in the official key.

questions.q: Article 338 of the Indian Constitution deals with?
questions.exp: Article 338 of the Indian Constitution deals with the National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC).

questions.q: Who among the following can impose the penalty under Section 20 of the Right to Information Act?
opts: Central Information Commission or State Information Commission
High Court or Central Information Commission or State Information Commission
questions.exp: Under Section 20 of the RTI Act, penalty can be imposed by the Central Information Commission (CIC) or the State Information Commission (SIC) as applicable.

questions.q: Comptroller and Auditor General of India submits its reports relating to accounts of the Union to?
questions.exp: Under Article 151, the CAG submits reports to the President of India (not VP or PM), who then causes them to be laid before each House of Parliament. None of the given options is correct.

questions.q: Chairperson and Members of the State Public Service Commission are appointed by?
questions.exp: Under Article 316 of the Indian Constitution, the Chairperson and members of the State Public Service Commission are appointed by the Governor of the state.

questions.q: The Development Monitoring and Evaluation Office (DMEO) was constituted on?
questions.exp: The Development Monitoring and Evaluation Office (DMEO) under NITI Aayog was constituted on 18 September 2015.

questions.q: Which of the following Governors of Madhya Pradesh have NOT been members of the Constituent Assembly of India?\n1. H. V. Pataskar  2. Niranjan Nath Wanchoo  3. Satyanarayan Sinha  4. K. C. Reddy
questions.exp: H. V. Pataskar and Niranjan Nath Wanchoo were not members of the Constituent Assembly of India while serving as Governors of MP.

questions.q: Who among the following Chief Ministers of Madhya Pradesh has NOT held the office of the leader of the house in MP Legislative Assembly during his tenure?
questions.exp: Govind Narayan Singh did not hold the office of leader of the house in the MP Legislative Assembly during his tenure as Chief Minister.

questions.q: Who among the following has held the office of the Protem Speaker, Speaker and Deputy Speaker in Madhya Pradesh Legislative Assembly?
questions.exp: Kunjilal Dube held all three offices — Protem Speaker, Speaker, and Deputy Speaker — of the Madhya Pradesh Legislative Assembly.

questions.q: Who will preside over the meeting of the Gram Sabha in the absence of the Sarpanch and Up-Sarpanch in Madhya Pradesh?
opts: The oldest person of the respective Gram Panchayat elected by members present
The Panch of the respective Gram Panchayat, elected by the members present in the Gram Sabha meeting
questions.exp: In the absence of the Sarpanch and Up-Sarpanch, the Panch of the Gram Panchayat elected by members present will preside over the Gram Sabha meeting in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Which of the following are the Standing Committees of Zila Panchayat in Madhya Pradesh?\n1. General Administration Committee  2. Agriculture Committee  3. Education Committee
questions.exp: All three — General Administration Committee, Agriculture Committee, and Education Committee — are Standing Committees of Zila Panchayat in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Which of the following Airports is/are NOT International Airports?\n1. Bhopal  2. Indore  3. Khajuraho  4. Gwalior
questions.exp: Khajuraho Airport is not an international airport. Bhopal (Raja Bhoj), Indore (Devi Ahilyabai Holkar), and Gwalior airports have international status.

questions.q: Which of the following place and district in Madhya Pradesh is NOT associated with a Thermal Power Station?
questions.exp: Dongaliya Mundi in Khargone is not associated with a Thermal Power Station. Sarani (Satpura TPS), Amarkantak, and Birsingpur (Sanjay Gandhi TPS, Umaria) all have thermal power stations.

questions.q: Which of the following pairs is NOT correctly matched?\n(Irrigation Project — District)
questions.exp: The Barna Ghati Project is NOT in Ujjain district. Barna Dam is located in Raisen district of Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q:
Consider the following statements:
1. Cotton textile mills have been established in Malwa Plateau of Madhya Pradesh.
2. Malwa Plateau is covered with black soil.
Select the correct answer:
questions.exp: Both statements are correct. Malwa Plateau has cotton textile mills (Indore, Ujjain) and is indeed covered with black (regur) soil suitable for cotton cultivation.

questions.q: In which year did the Bhopal Gas disaster occur?
questions.exp: The Bhopal Gas Tragedy occurred on the night of 2-3 December 1984, when methyl isocyanate gas leaked from the Union Carbide India Limited (UCIL) pesticide plant.

questions.q: In which organ are Sertoli cells found?
questions.exp: Sertoli cells are found in the seminiferous tubules of the testis. They are also called 'nurse cells' and play a vital role in spermatogenesis.

questions.q: Where is the UNICEF Headquarters situated?
questions.exp: UNICEF (United Nations Children's Fund) Headquarters is located in New York City, USA.

questions.q: In which organ is hydrochloric acid secreted?
questions.exp: Hydrochloric acid (HCl) is secreted by the parietal cells (oxyntic cells) of the gastric glands in the stomach.

questions.q: Scurvy disease is caused by deficiency of?
questions.exp: Scurvy is caused by deficiency of Vitamin C (ascorbic acid), which is essential for collagen synthesis.

questions.q: The words 'To Abide and Respect' used under Article 51A of the Constitution of India are concerned with?
opts: The sovereignty, unity and integrity of India
Rich heritage of our composite culture
questions.exp: Article 51A(a) of the Indian Constitution states it is a duty of every citizen to abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem.

questions.q: Which of the following Constitutional Amendments was the first to be ratified by the State Legislatures as per the requirements of Article 368(2)?
questions.exp: The Third Constitutional Amendment Act, 1954 was the first amendment to be ratified by the required number of State Legislatures as per Article 368(2).

questions.q: The power of the Supreme Court of India to decide disputes between Centre and States falls under which Jurisdiction?
questions.exp: Under Article 131, the Supreme Court has Original Jurisdiction in disputes between the Centre and States or between States themselves.

questions.q: According to Economic Survey 2020–21, the real growth rate for financial year 2022 is assumed as (based on IMF estimates)?
questions.exp: The Economic Survey 2020-21 projected India's real GDP growth rate for FY 2021-22 at 11.0% (based on various estimates). IMF projected 9.5% — making option C correct as per the paper.

questions.q: Mid Day Meal Scheme was launched in which year?
questions.exp: The Mid Day Meal Scheme was launched in India in 1995 by the Government of India to improve nutritional status and school attendance of children.
